change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: The project aims to promote women’s rights, quality education, and resilient communities by addressing systemic inequalities and enhancing community capacities. It raises awareness and builds capacity among women’s groups, activists, and government officials on unpaid care work, gender-responsive budgeting, and programming, while fostering dialogue to advance women-friendly policies. Initiatives include advocacy, campaigns, and evidence generation to reduce gender-based violence (GBV), support survivors, and challenge harmful norms. It strengthens women’s and adolescent girls' agency through advocacy, capacity building, and community mobilization, enabling them to claim rights and challenge structural inequalities.The project also focuses on education, supporting inclusive school infrastructure, teacher training, and eco-friendly initiatives, while engaging child and youth clubs in conservation and resilience-building activities. Evidence-based advocacy for gender-responsive public services and equitable education financing is promoted through partnerships with civil society, unions, and local governments.In building resilience, the project empowers farmers with skills for climate-adaptive agroecology, links them to markets, and supports climate change advocacy. It facilitates disaster risk reduction (DRR) efforts, including establishing early warning systems, prepositioning relief materials, and preparing gender-sensitive guidelines for humanitarian response. By fostering partnerships, generating evidence, and mobilizing stakeholders, the project drives systemic change toward gender equality, inclusive education, and community resilience.

snapshot.executing_agency: Ministry of Women, Children and Senior Citizens, Ministry of Land Management, Cooperatives and Poverty Alleviation, Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Development, Ministry of Education, Science and Technology

en.full: Enhancing socio-economic condition of deprived communities through collective actions

ne.full: Enhancing socio-economic condition of deprived communities through collective actions
